body_text: Colocation revenue is 100% attributable to a single customer. The relationship is financed by a $3.3B project bond at 7.75%, and that same customer is also the lender's collateral. When one tenant is both your entire business and your lender's security, "diversified" is not the word that applies.

Colocation revenue reached $77.5M in Q1. For the first time it exceeded the company's own bitcoin mining revenue, the segment the business was built on. The filing reports the figure; it does not characterize what it means for the rest of the year.

Sell-side analysts estimate that the next architecture roughly doubles memory bandwidth again. This is an analyst estimate, not a company figure. Prior estimates of this kind have been directionally right before, and early on timing before.

Lead times for large power transformers remain 128-144 weeks. That is roughly three years to obtain a component every new datacenter needs. The figure has not moved; nothing in the tracker indicates it panicked anyone.
Only three companies on earth currently manufacture HBM at scale. The open question is whether anyone else can reach scale production. So far, no fourth maker has.

The single-customer colocation relationship is financed by a $3.3B project bond at 7.75%. That same customer is also the lender's collateral. When one tenant is at once the entire business and the security behind the debt, the word "diversified" does not apply.
